notes: This file defines the current 197 fields from the top100 schema.
Priority P0 = mandatory for top100 hero tools; P1 = strongly recommended; P2 = later/long-tail enrichment.
Use derived page payloads rather than passing the full record to every Astro page.

id.description: Canonical internal tool identifier. Must stay stable once assigned.

name.description: Raw official product name from the vendor.

slug.description: Canonical URL-safe slug used across the site.

display_name.description: Human-facing product name shown in headings and cards.

aliases.description: Alternate names, abbreviations, or previous brand names.

short_description.description: Concise description for cards, intros, and snippets.

long_description.description: Richer description for tool pages and intro sections.

icon_url.description: Compact icon or favicon-style asset URL.

docs_url.description: Official documentation or help center URL.

company_name.description: Operating company name behind the product.

categories.description: Allowed multi-category assignments for discovery and linking.

subcategories.description: Allowed multi-subcategory assignments for discovery and linking.

microcategories.description: Fine-grained taxonomy labels used for long-tail directory pages.

tags.description: Curated flexible discovery labels; avoid spammy over-tagging.

use_cases.description: Specific outcome-driven user tasks the tool helps complete.

capabilities.description: Normalized product capabilities used for discovery and compare logic.

industries.description: Industries where the tool has a credible fit.

roles.description: Roles that directly benefit from the tool.

personas.description: Softer audience labels such as agencies, creators, or students.

departments.description: Business departments where the tool is relevant.

ai_models.description: Named AI or LLM models supported or used by the product.

integrations.description: Normalized integration slugs for ecosystem and workflow pages.

platforms.description: Supported platforms like web, API, iOS, Android, Chrome extension, desktop.

skill_levels.description: Normalized difficulty or maturity fit labels.

core_features.description: Neutral core features the product offers.

headline_features.description: Most important standout features shown high on page.

unique_features.description: Genuinely differentiating features, not generic claims.

trial_days.description: Trial duration in days where clearly stated.

pro_price_monthly.description: Typical best-fit plan for solo pros.

team_price_monthly.description: Typical collaboration or team plan price.

pricing_notes.description: Important caveats like limits, credits, or hidden add-ons.

money_value_score.description: Derived estimate of value for money.

pricing_transparency_score.description: How clear and understandable the pricing is.

rating_count.description: Primary rating count used with rating_average.

review_count_total.description: Total review count aggregated across trusted sources where possible.

score_integration_strength.description: Depth and breadth of integration ecosystem.

score_value_for_money.description: Relative value considering price, output, and limits.

score_workflow_fit.description: How useful the tool is inside real workflows.

score_trust.description: Trust score from company and review signals.

score_geo_value.description: Expected value for generative engine citation/discovery.

score_compare_value.description: How worthwhile this tool is in compare-page programs.

score_bestof_value.description: How suitable the tool is for best-of list inclusion.

primary_keyword.description: Core keyword target for the tool page.

secondary_keywords.description: High-intent support terms around the core keyword.

entity_keywords.description: Entity-rich labels for SEO, GEO, and AI citation relevance.

serp_intents.description: Search intents the content should satisfy.

breadcrumbs.description: Structured breadcrumb path derived from taxonomy.

faq_items.description: Fact-grounded FAQ items for tool pages.

snippet_candidates.description: Short answer-style statements for featured snippets and AI overviews.

llm_answer_targets.description: Specific questions the page should answer well for answer engines.

primary_competitors.description: Closest direct competitors by intent and buyer overlap.

compare_candidates.description: Tools that should be prioritized for direct compare pages.

alternatives.description: Tools suitable for alternatives lists and related widgets.

unique_selling_points.description: Short differentiators used in intros and comparison blurbs.

needs_enrichment.description: Whether the tool still needs significant data work.

schema_version.description: Schema version used for the record.

source_priority.description: Source provenance by field or source group.
